action.hypothesis: JumpReLU at the K=128 quality anchor may be undertrained at the current signum learning rate, and lowering LR to 4e-4 could improve optimization stability enough to beat the current 0.1888 full-FVU baseline.
action.summary: Return to the best-quality JumpReLU K=128 configuration and start a systematic LR sweep with 4e-4, keeping all other settings fixed so the round cleanly tests whether optimization, not architecture, is limiting quality.
action.self_review: This keeps the round narrowly focused on a single LR change on the strongest quality anchor after completing the required K ladder. The main risk is that lower LR may simply slow progress without improving FVU, but it is the cleanest next exploitation step given that K=64 and K=32 both trailed the K=128 baseline.
notes_to_memory: Completed the required K exploration ladder: K=64 and K=32 both remained workable but did not beat the K=128 quality anchor.
Round 11 returns to the best-quality JumpReLU K=128 setup and begins the requested single-parameter LR sweep.
Testing LR=4e-4 first probes whether the current baseline is limited by overly aggressive signum updates rather than by model capacity or sparsity budget.
next_hypotheses: If LR=4e-4 improves proxy FVU, promote to full and continue LR refinement around the K=128 anchor before revisiting smaller K.
If LR=4e-4 is flat or worse, test LR=1.6e-3 next on the same K=128 JumpReLU baseline to probe the opposite optimization direction.
If LR sweeps fail to move quality, keep K=128 fixed and sweep auxk_alpha next, starting with 0 or 0.01.
